claim.observation: istat of MFT entry 124824 reports 'Not Allocated File' with a $DATA Actual Size of 862864067 bytes (~823 MB). Two recorded names are the 8.3 name 'WINDOZ~1.ZZZ' and the long name 'windoZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZ.ZZZ'; both have Parent MFT Entry: 5. Created 2018-09-05 14:32:36 (UTC), File Modified 2018-09-06 16:33:25 (UTC). icat returned 0 bytes of recoverable data (recovered_bytes=0).
claim.interpretation: Inode 124824 is a deleted ~823 MB file located in the NTFS root directory (Parent MFT Entry 5 is the volume root), i.e. at C:\. Its name has been changed to a non-descriptive run of repeated 'Z' characters with a .ZZZ extension, and icat recovers no data, so its content is overwritten and unrecoverable. Its creation (2018-09-05 14:32) and modification (2018-09-06 16:33) fall in the same window as the deleted WinRAR tooling (finding F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-001) and the ~194 MB deleted file (F-analyst-SRL18-FILE-DISK-003). This is a second large file with an obfuscated name and overwritten content. The finding establishes the file's existence, size, root location, timing, name change, and content-overwrite only -- not the responsible tool or actor, the original contents, nor any transfer off the host.

evidence_pointer.artifact: base-file-cdrive.E01 -- NTFS volume (offset 0), deleted file inode 124824 at volume root (C:\)
evidence_pointer.locator: MFT 124824; Parent MFT Entry 5 (root); $DATA actual size 862864067; Created 2018-09-05 14:32:36 UTC
evidence_pointer.command: istat -o 0 IMG 124824 | grep -E 'Not Allocated File|Actual Size|Created:|File Modified:|Name:|Parent MFT Entry:' ; echo -n recovered_bytes= ; icat -o 0 IMG 124824 | wc -c
